content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

September 6, 1995


Diane M. Fishburn
Olson, Hagel, Fong, Leidigh,
Waters & Fishburn
555 Capitol Mall, Ste. 1425
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Confirmation
of Telephone Advice
Our File No. I-95-260

Dear Ms. Fishburn:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
to you concerning the campaign disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/ Because your inquiry is
general in nature, we will treat your letter as a request for
informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c).2/

Your letter dated August 9, 1995, accurately summarizes my
advice concerning the filing of supplemental pre-election campaign
statements pursuant to Section 84202.5 by a legislator who has
more than one controlled committee. According to your facts, one
of the legislator's committees made contributions to support
candidates or committees in connection with the August 22, 1995,
recall election in the 17th Assembly District and was required to
file a supplemental pre-election statement. The other committee
controlled by the legislator made no contributions in connection
with the election. I advised you that the committee which made no

____________
1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)


428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660

Diane M. Fishburn
Our File No. I-95-260
Page 2


contributions in connection with the election would not be
required to file a supplemental pre-election statement.

If you have questions concerning this letter, please contact
me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Carla Wardlow
Carla Wardlow
Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division

sections.question_synthetic: Must a legislator with multiple controlled committees file supplemental pre-election campaign statements for all committees, or only those committees that made contributions in connection with a recall election?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: A committee controlled by a legislator that made no contributions in connection with an election is not required to file a supplemental pre-election statement under Section 84202.5, even if another committee controlled by the same legislator did make such contributions and was required to file.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is informal advice confirming telephone guidance. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present; information extracted from letter body. Requestor is attorney Diane M. Fishburn seeking confirmation of prior oral advice regarding Political Reform Act compliance.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Must a legislator with multiple controlled committees file supplemental pre-election campaign statements for all committees, or only those committees that made contributions in connection with a recall election?

CONCLUSION: A committee controlled by a legislator that made no contributions in connection with an election is not required to file a supplemental pre-election statement under Section 84202.5, even if another committee controlled by the same legislator did make such contributions and was required to file.

embedding.summary: FPPC confirms prior telephone advice to counsel regarding supplemental pre-election campaign statement filing requirements for a legislator with multiple controlled committees, clarifying that only committees making contributions in connection with the election must file such statements.
